premise: After my husband died, I poured my entire $200,000 widow's pension into launching a bed-and-breakfast with his son from his first marriage, trusting him to honor a 50/50 handshake deal, but he forged documents claiming I owned just 10%, forced a sale the day after my 60th birthday, and pocketed $275,000 in cash. I spent two years uncovering the forgery, sued for fraud and elder abuse, won a $320,000 judgment that forced him to sell his own house, and used the money to open a rival inn across the lake that bankrupted his new ‘luxury lodge’ within a season.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Stepson Forged My Name, Stole My Pension, And Sold My Inn. So I Opened One Ac
